stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the national interest.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white middle class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the national interest. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
